mentions.evidence: The free trial alone will give you a huge amount of content
I really do like gollum
buy it on this mega spring sale and dont bother paying 40 bucks for it
the controls are a bit finnicky
the AI threshold is honestly quite stupid
The way some of the stealth missions are set up is extremely annoying, as you cannot automatically sneak coming off a ladder or ledge, so often times the enemy will hear you and alert everyone else.
the graphics are honestly quite beautiful. coming off of games like bg3 and elden ring, I;d say the backgrounds are similarly detailed and a feast for the eyes
the character models are the UGLIEST things ive seen in a bit.

annotations.note: Character-model complaint routed to art_style (design/look, 'bug-eyed cocomelon kid', 'looks off') rather than folding into the graphics mention, since the charge reads as aesthetic design rather than rendering fidelity — a judgment call.

mentions.evidence: Crap optimistion.

games visuals are muddy as hell even on max settings
The fact I have to stream the game for it to run is crazy.
Do the devs even bother patching this any more?
I absolutely love the environment and the style of the game.
I absolutely love the environment and the style of the game.
I love the great stealth
the emotions that were there
he should own your soul. The game succeeds at that mission
over the years of constant expansions and updates, it has become exceedingly efficient at it

annotations.note: The 'Free to Play... but how much will it cost you? Everything' opening reads as a rhetorical setup resolved by the soul-owning line into an addictiveness charge, not a separate monetization charge — no real-money complaint follows, so monetization was not labeled. Worth confirming.
Too terse/ambiguous a referent ('my friend') to tell whether this names a companion character, a co-op partner, or something outside the game entirely — left unlabeled rather than guessing.

mentions.evidence: What's not to love about this game, from the characters, to the tasks, to the visuals you get; theres always something that'll catch your eye.

annotations.note: The 'from the characters, to the tasks, to the visuals' list was read as three independently-praised properties (each filling the 'what's not to love' slot) rather than bare enumerated contributors folding into one mention — the reviewer-folded-enumeration ruling's dividing line is close here.
